title: SciAtlas: A Large-Scale Knowledge Graph for Automated Scientific Research
optimized_headline: "Discover SciAtlas: A Revolutionary Knowledge Graph Transforming Scientific Research"

raw_body:
arXiv:2605.22878v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The exponential growth of global academic output has confronted researchers and AI agents with an unprecedented ``information explosion,'' where fragmented and unstructured knowledge organization impedes deep interdisciplinary integration. Current academic retrieval tools predominantly rely on superficial keyword matching or vector-space semantic retrieval, which lack the topological reasoning capabilities required to navigate complex logical connections. Agentic deep-research-based frameworks are often prone to logical hallucinations and consuming high inference costs. To bridge this gap, in this report, we introduce SciAtlas, a large-scale, multi-disciplinary, heterogeneous academic resource knowledge graph designed as a panoramic scientific evolution network. By integrating over 43M papers from 26 disciplines, and a total of 157M entities and 3B triplets, SciAtlas provides a structured topological cognitive substrate that dismantles disciplinary barriers and furnishes AI agents with a global perspective. Furthermore, we develop a neuro-symbolic retrieval algorithm featuring tri-path collaborative recall and graph reranking, achieving a seamless transition from simple semantic matching to deterministic association discovery. We also present key application directions of SciAtlas, including literature review, automated research trend synthesis, idea positioning, and academic trajectory exploration, to demonstrate that SciAtlas can serve as an effective ``cognitive map'' to empower the full loop of automated scientific research while significantly reducing reasoning costs. We have released the interfaces for KG retrieval and various downstream tasks in our GitHub repo.
speedrun: SciAtlas is a newly introduced knowledge graph that aims to tackle the overwhelming amount of academic information available today. By integrating over 43 million papers and 157 million entities, it provides a structured way for AI to navigate complex scientific connections. This is crucial as traditional tools struggle with deep interdisciplinary research, often leading to inefficiencies and inaccuracies. SciAtlas could significantly enhance how researchers and AI approach scientific inquiries.
why_it_matters: Researchers can now access a more organized and comprehensive resource, making interdisciplinary studies more feasible and efficient.
This development indicates a shift towards more sophisticated AI tools that can better support complex academic research, potentially transforming how knowledge is synthesized.

lenses.eli12: SciAtlas is like a detailed map for scientists, helping them find their way through a vast landscape of information. Instead of getting lost in a sea of papers, researchers can use this tool to see connections and trends clearly. This matters because it could make scientific research faster and more effective for everyone.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, SciAtlas highlights a growing user need for more advanced research tools that cut through information clutter. Its ability to streamline literature reviews and synthesize trends could save time and resources, allowing teams to focus on innovation. This could drive the development of new applications that leverage its capabilities.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, SciAtlas employs a neuro-symbolic retrieval algorithm that enhances traditional semantic matching with deterministic association discovery. This approach utilizes a vast dataset of 3 billion triplets to improve logical reasoning and reduce inference costs. Such advancements could set new benchmarks for knowledge graphs in academic research.
